model.label: MiMo-V2-Pro
model.description: MiMo • The flagship foundation for the agentic era, built for complex, long-horizon, multi-step agentic tasks.

pick.rationale: Gonzaga holds a significant analytical edge with superior KenPom (11 vs 23) and Torvik (11 vs 33) rankings, featuring elite defensive efficiency (rank 9) that should contain BYU's potent offense. While BYU has a strong offensive profile (rank 10), the season-ending ACL injury to star guard Richie Saunders substantially weakens their firepower and matchup versatility. Gonzaga's own injury to Braden Huff is a concern, but the Bulldogs' overall talent depth, defensive prowess, and tournament experience under Mark Few provide a crucial edge in this Round of 32 matchup.

reasoningStep.title: BYU vs Gonzaga Round of 32 Analysis
reasoningStep.summary: Gonzaga favored due to superior defensive metrics and BYU's key injury, though both teams have significant absences.
evidence: Gonzaga ranks 11th in both KenPom and Torvik with a 30-3 record, while BYU ranks 23rd/33rd with a 23-11 record.
Gonzaga's defensive efficiency (rank 9) is significantly better than BYU's (rank 88), creating a key matchup advantage.
BYU's star guard Richie Saunders is out for the tournament with a torn ACL, substantially weakening their offensive firepower.
Gonzaga's Braden Huff is also out with a dislocated kneecap, but the Bulldogs have more overall depth to compensate.
BYU's offensive efficiency (rank 10) is elite, but without Saunders, their ability to execute against Gonzaga's defense is compromised.
Gonzaga's tournament experience and coaching under Mark Few provide an edge in high-pressure March games.

modelTrace.content:
You are an analyst predicting a single March Madness game inside a bracket run.
You have live tools. Use them before making a pick when they can reduce uncertainty.
You are not filling the whole bracket in one response. Predict only the current game.
Use prior picks as already committed bracket state.
You may use at most 10 tool rounds before you must finalize your answer.
Before making a pick, investigate relevant context such as injuries or availability concerns, recent team news, prior head-to-head results when useful, and multiple raw statistics from the available ratings and matchup data.
Take a holistic approach. Balance team quality, matchup specifics, health, form, schedule context, and upset risk instead of relying on one metric.
Do not force an upset, but explicitly consider whether the underdog has a credible path to win and let that affect confidence and rationale.
Return strict JSON only.
Final JSON keys: pick, reasoningStep.
pick must contain gameId, winnerId, confidence, rationale.
The rationale must be a short paragraph of 2 to 4 sentences explaining why the winner was chosen.
reasoningStep must contain id, title, summary, evidence.
